Author: Vincy A
Title: What makes a standout software engineer in 2025, at a company heavily using AI coding tools? Here's what Charles-Axel Dein, engineering manager at Cloud Kitchens says he looks for. It's not what I'd… | Gergely Orosz | 11 comments
Text:
What makes a standout software engineer in 2025, at a company heavily using AI coding tools? Here's what Charles-Axel Dein, engineering manager at Cloud Kitchens says he looks for. It's not what I'd wager many people expect, given there's little to no mention about AI! Charles hired me at Uber (thanks!) and was my first manager there, and I "copied" a lot of practical approaches from him in my own career. He is also the author of the Professional Programming Reading List GitHub repo, that he's been maintaining for 15 years, and is at 49,000 stars: https://lnkd.in/eumZTF_V The full podcast episode with Charles: • YouTube: https://lnkd.in/exmU79tc • Spotify: https://lnkd.in/esXJcCcB • Apple: https://lnkd.in/e7VCjqqW • Summary and transcript: https://lnkd.in/eH9VuPKi
focusing on fundamentals like communication ownership and learning makes it easier to solve the complex problems that ai alone cannot fix
Really interesting perspective. The tools evolve, but the craft remains, empathy, rigor, and taste still separate great engineers from good ones.
Exactly. When tools compress the coding part, what stands out is how engineers think, simplify, and shape decisions.
Great stuff. For me, as a leader, great developers need curiosity, judgment, and the ability to understand systems end-to-end. AI can’t replace clear thinking and good instincts.
Self deprecation is so important - it is to show that you are also human and makes mistakes! Also important, how you handle mistakes so you build a psychological safe environment All that is connected and it is amazing
Standing out isn't about knowing the latest tech. It's about solving problems when no one else can and carrying your team to success. Gergely Orosz
"The ability to self deprecate" - Amazing articulation.
what does it mean by self-deprecate?
